articles.title: Bluon: The HVAC Tech Brain in Every Technician's Pocket.
articles.deck: Bluon puts every manual, wiring diagram, parts list, and AI diagnostic for 30 million HVAC model numbers into a technician's pocket. For LA multifamily owners, fewer callbacks. For small HVAC shops, it's the difference between competing and not.
articles.hero_alt: Three smartphone screens showing the Bluon HVAC app with model lookup, manuals, and parts screens.

articles.title: 1101 W 45th, 90037: A 20-Door Trade at a 6.79 Cap.
articles.deck: A 20-unit Historic South Central multifamily just traded at $2,250,000. Numbers line up tight: 6.79% CAP, sub-7 GRM, under $200 a foot, $112K a door. And the broker who got it done is worth writing about.
articles.hero_alt: 1101 W 45th Street, a two-story Spanish-tile-roof 20-unit apartment building in Historic South Central Los Angeles.

articles.title: 5712 Camellia, NoHo: 14 Doors at $306K a Unit.
articles.deck: A 1989 NoHo apartment just hit the market at $4.295M. $306,786/door, 4.86% CAP current, 12.23 GRM. Not RSO, which is the whole pitch. An operator's read on whether the ask clears.
articles.hero_alt: 5712 Camellia Ave, a three-story stucco 14-unit apartment building in North Hollywood.

articles.title: 1825 N Gramercy: A 27-Door Hollywood Trade at $187K a Unit.
articles.deck: A 1968 Hollywood multifamily just closed at $5.05M, 9.3 GRM, $174/SF, priced below the ULA line. The kind of deal where disciplined leverage, operating patience, and sensible policy reform can produce a 17% IRR over a decade.
articles.hero_alt: 1825 N Gramercy Pl, a 1968 three-story Hollywood apartment building with tuck-under parking and mature palm trees.
